concepts.name: explicit-schema batch write
limitations: no schema inference
columns must match schema order
all columns must have equal length

concepts.name: row-group column read
limitations: input stream must be seekable
read by row group then column
not row-object oriented

concepts.name: batched column read
limitations: BatchSize only reduces memory with ReadColumnBatches APIs
ReadColumn still returns the full row-group column

concepts.name: target framework date behavior
limitations: DateOnly is unavailable on netstandard2.0
netstandard2.0 date columns materialize as DateTime

apis.intendedUsage: Create a new parquet file from explicit schema-ordered column batches.
apis.threading: No thread-safety guarantee; coordinate access externally.
performanceNotes: batch column arrays instead of row-at-a-time writes
use MaxRowGroupRows or MaxRowGroupBytes for row-group sizing
NativeWriteBatchSize tunes parquet-rs encoder chunking only and defaults to 8192 rows in this library
use page-size and adaptive native writer memory options for lower peak native buffers
limitations: no append-to-existing-file API
no schema inference
no async API

apis.intendedUsage: Open a seekable parquet stream and create row-group scoped readers.
apis.threading: No thread-safety guarantee; coordinate access externally.
limitations: input stream must be seekable
no async API

apis.intendedUsage: Read selected columns from one parquet row group.
apis.threading: No thread-safety guarantee; coordinate access externally.
limitations: ReadColumn<T> requires exact CLR type
ReadColumn returns the full selected row-group column
ReadColumnBatches is required for lower peak memory
BatchSize chunks returned arrays while row-range overloads limit selected input rows
row-range reads are scoped to the opened row group
decimal reads use SqlDecimal
date reads depend on target framework

apis.intendedUsage: Configure batched column reads to reduce peak memory for large row-group columns.
limitations: BatchSize must be greater than zero
BatchSize does not change full-column ReadColumn return shape

apis.intendedUsage: Configure parquet writer behavior for a file creation operation.
limitations: NativeWriteBatchSize does not split managed WriteBatch calls
NativeWriteBatchSize does not define data page size or row-group boundaries
MaxNativeWriterMemoryBytes is checked after each managed WriteBatch and is not a hard process memory cap
smaller page and row-group settings can increase file size and metadata overhead
callers control managed batch sizes
